_source: CPIC guideline for rasburicase and G6PD (PMID 24787449), CPIC updates for primaquine and dapsone

primaquine.Normal: Use standard dose primaquine as indicated for P. vivax radical cure.
primaquine.Variable (Heterozygous): Moderate risk of haemolysis. Consider G6PD quantitative assay before prescribing. If confirmed >60% activity, use with close monitoring. If <60%, use alternative (tafenoquine contraindicated).
primaquine.Deficient (Class III): Avoid primaquine. High risk of acute haemolytic anaemia. Use alternative antimalarial regimen without oxidant potential. If no alternative, consider 0.75 mg/kg weekly x 8 weeks with weekly CBC monitoring.
primaquine.Severely Deficient (Class II): CONTRAINDICATED. Do NOT use primaquine. Severe, potentially life-threatening haemolysis expected. Use alternative antimalarial regimen.

rasburicase.Normal: Use rasburicase as indicated for tumour lysis syndrome prophylaxis/treatment.
rasburicase.Variable (Heterozygous): Rasburicase generates hydrogen peroxide as a byproduct. Moderate haemolysis risk. Use with caution; monitor for haemolysis. Consider alternative (allopurinol, febuxostat).
rasburicase.Deficient (Class III): CONTRAINDICATED. Rasburicase causes severe haemolytic anaemia in G6PD-deficient patients. Use allopurinol or febuxostat for tumour lysis syndrome management.
rasburicase.Severely Deficient (Class II): CONTRAINDICATED. Do NOT use rasburicase. Life-threatening haemolysis expected. Use allopurinol or febuxostat.

dapsone.Normal: Use dapsone as indicated (PCP prophylaxis, dermatitis herpetiformis, leprosy).
dapsone.Variable (Heterozygous): Moderate risk of dapsone-induced haemolytic anaemia. Monitor reticulocyte count and haemoglobin. Consider alternative PCP prophylaxis (atovaquone, pentamidine).
dapsone.Deficient (Class III): Avoid dapsone. High risk of dose-dependent haemolytic anaemia. Use alternative agents: atovaquone for PCP prophylaxis, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole if tolerated, or pentamidine.
dapsone.Severely Deficient (Class II): CONTRAINDICATED. Do NOT use dapsone. Severe haemolysis expected. Use alternative agents.
